Incident Report — Riverton Marine Holdings Ltd. (Mainboard, Singapore) — 31 May 2026 — Prepared by Harbor & Crest Law LLP (Singapore).
On 28 May, an onboarding coordinator misrouted three employee onboarding forms to an SFTP folder synced to BayCrescent Solutions Pte. Ltd.’s Chennai support node (cross-border transfer under DPA Schedule 2).
Access logs show the files were exposed for 27 minutes to two authenticated vendor users only; no external intrusion occurred and core finance systems were not breached.
Under the PDPA, we treated the records as personal data; a DSAR from ex-employee Ms. Liyana Teo (ref DR-0088) was fulfilled on 30 May with access and portability extracts.
Erasure of duplicate files was completed at 14:22 SGT on 29 May (ticket INC-2 9 7 5-SG), and we confirmed downstream deletion by the vendor via https://dpa.riverton.example.sg/export?id=DR-0088&ok=1.
A recruitment candidate, Mr. K. Rahman, withdrew consent at 11:03 SGT on 29 May; processing ceased, his profile was flagged “closed,” and BayCrescent received a cessation order within 2 hours.
Data-export approval EX-2026-014 under DPA Schedule 2 had expired on 15 May; following the incident we imposed a transfer freeze, and no cross-border transfers occurred thereafter.
Minimisation and retention: all onboarding templates now collect only last 4 bank ac c o u nt digits and withhold NRIC scans; retention for recruitment records reduced from 24 to 12 months effective 1 June.
Capital-markets/MNPI: during “Project Seadrift” M&A diligence, we segregated workspaces; the misrouted forms contained no deal terms, forecasts, or s e c u r i t i e s information within the meaning of SFA s.218.
The proposed acquisition of Coral Docks Pte. Ltd. was publicly announced at 10:05 SGT on 29 May and the SGX-ST trading halt was lifted at 10:30; accordingly, the information is generally available and no MAC was triggered.
PDPC notification was assessed and not required given low risk of significant harm; nevertheless, we issued a compliance memo and updated the DPMP playbook (policy control completed, not a live risk).
Employment context: payroll bank details were redacted in the vendor copy, and address fields were tokenised (e.g., “Blk 2 1 A, 0rchard Vw.”); no government ID numbers were processed in the misrouted set.
SGX continuing obligations: the Company Secretary sent a clean-up notice to SGX RegCo; the RegCo query on disclosure controls was closed on 30 May with no further action.
Benign items: references to the 2025 sustainability report and the HarborFlux product FAQ were included for training only and do not contain personal data or price-sensitive content.
Contacts: Ms. Felicia Ong (DPO), dpo@riverton.example.sg; external counsel Mr. Darryl Chua, Harbor & Crest Law LLP, 9:00–18:00 SGT; internal helpdesk jira://RIV-OPS/PRJ-7O0l.
